Meaning: Tests whether the activity / artefact substantively falls within the law (what?).
Tests whether the law is in force / a provision is applicable at the relevant time (when?).
Tests whether the situation has a jurisdictional link to the EU (where?).
Hard carve-out — when satisfied, the law does not apply at all to the scenario.
Member-State or context-specific derogation that modifies obligations without removing scope.
Defines a term needed to evaluate scope (e.g. 'personal data', 'AI system').
Defines an actor type (controller, provider, deployer, etc.). Roles attach obligations and anchor territorial scope.
Tests that classify an artefact (high-risk AI, special-category data, GPAI with systemic risk, etc.).
Top-level rules that combine material + temporal + territorial - exclusions to decide applies/3.
Hand-encoded list (Annex I, Annex III) referenced by classification rules.
Provision flagged for human review — kept in the corpus but no rules generated yet.
Short form ID for each Articles+Rules row: G001..Gnnn for GDPR, A001..Annn for AI Act. Independent of provision_id.
F001..Fnnn — short ID for each predicate row in Required facts.
N001..Nnnn — KG node-class identifier.
E001..Ennn — KG relationship identifier.
P001..Pnnn — KG property/label identifier.
World asserts it. Extracted by the LLM into the company playbook KG.
Engine derives it from rules. Written back as derived labels / reified events. Do NOT extract.
Static lookup data (Annex I, Annex III, entry-into-force dates). Engine-seeded.
Arity-1 type predicate (processing/1, natural_person/1) — sets a node label.
Arity-1 flag predicate (automated_means/1) — sets a boolean property on the node.
Arity-2 predicate (controller/2) — becomes a relationship between two nodes.
Arity-3+ predicate (applies/3) — materialises as a node with edges to its arguments.
Intensional arity-1 predicate (high_risk_ai/1) — engine-written label.
